Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC's And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant SVES GO, LLC's motion to compel further responses to its set one special interrogatories is granted in part and its request for monetary sanction is denied. Gap must serve verified code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 3 and 5 without objections no later than January 29, 2026. The motion is not untimely. The fairer and better reading of Allender's July 10, 2025 4:09pm and August 4, 2025 7:23am emails is that Gap agreed to extend the deadline for the filing of this motion to 45 days after Gap's anticipated amended responses to SVES's first sets of form and special interrogatories and requests for admission. Although Gap never served amended responses to any of that discovery, it never disclaimed in writing that it would do so. Gap's oppression and overbreadth objections to special interrogatories 1 and 4 are sustained. Gap's objections to special interrogatories 3 and 5 lack merit and are overruled. Neither side acted without substantial justification, thereby precluding any award of monetary sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant SVES GO, LLC's motion to compel further responses to its set one form interrogatories is granted in part and its request for monetary sanction is denied. Gap must serve verified code-compliant further responses to form 17.1 regarding RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 without objections no later than January 29, 2026. The motion is not untimely. The fairer and better reading of Allender's July 10, 2025 4:09pm and August 4, 2025 7:23am emails is that Gap agreed to extend the deadline for the filing of this motion to 45 days after Gap's anticipated amended responses to SVES's first sets of form and special interrogatories and requests for admission. Although Gap never served amended responses to any of that discovery, it never disclaimed in writing that it would do so. Gap's lack of discovery relevance objection to form interrogatories 3.6 and 3.7 are sustained. Gap's responses to form interrogatories 4.1 and 4.2 are responsive and sufficient. Given the disparate allegations of wrongdoing in the complaint and cross-complaint, form interrogatories 12.1, 12.2 and 12.3 which are based on the word "incident" are not appropriate for this case. Gap's objections to form interrogatory 17.1 regarding RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 lack merit and are overruled. Gap's responses to form interrogatory 17.1 regarding RFAs 3-4, 7-8 and 11-18 are responsive and sufficient. Neither side acted without substantial justification, thereby precluding any award of monetary sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant SVES GO, LLC's motion to compel further responses to its set one requests for admission is granted in part and its request for monetary sanction is denied. Gap must serve verified code-compliant further responses to RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 without objections no later than January 29, 2026. The motion is not untimely. The fairer and better reading of Allender's July 10, 2025 4:09pm and August 4, 2025 7:23am emails is that Gap agreed to extend the deadline for the filing of this motion to 45 days after Gap's anticipated amended responses to SVES's first sets of form and special interrogatories and requests for admission. Although Gap never served amended responses to any of that discovery, it never disclaimed in writing that it would do so. Gap's objections to RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 lack merit and are overruled. Gap's responses to RFAs 3 and 4 when read in conjunction with its response to form interrogatory 17.1 regarding those RFAs are responsive and sufficient. Gap's response to RFA 6 is evasive because it does not respond to the RFA in its most likely intended way of asking if Gap was aware that SVES was preparing to go public through an IPO at the time that SVES was making preparations to go public through an IPO. Gap's response to RFA 10 is non-responsive. Neither side acted without substantial justification, thereby precluding any award of monetary sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT THE GAP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant's demurrer to all seven causes of action in the cross-complaint is overruled.

Cross-defendant fails to show that the settlement agreement bars this action. Even if the court takes judicial notice of the existence of the agreement, there is a dispute regarding its enforceability. That disputed issue cannot be resolved on demurrer. (Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4 th 1137 [court could not take judicial notice of written agreement that allegedly barred oral agreement].) A demurrer is " 'simply not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts.' " (Joslin v. H.A.S. Insurance Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 369, 374.) The court accepts cross-complainant's allegations of fraud, duress, and lack of consideration as true. The demurrer to causes of action five and six for tortious interference sufficiently alleges "wrongful conduct" outside the bounds of healthy competition. (Della Penna v. Toyota Motor Sales, U.S.A., Inc. (1995) 11 Cal.4th 376, 393; Cross-Complaint, pars. 8, 12, 15, 17.) The cross-
complaint also sufficiently alleges a violation of the UCL.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)

Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DANIEL ALLENDER, Esq. of Robins Kaplan LLP (tel: 310-552-0130) for Plaintiff The Gap, Inc.
Atty. SAM VAHEDI, Esq. of SVA Law Group (tel: 310-321-4616) for Defendant Sves Go, LLC.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO VACATE AND DECLARE VOID THE RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER ON 7/8/2024.

The Court holds a discussion regardig defendant's counsel failure to timely contest the tentative ruling. The Court allows oral argument, as both sides are present. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

SVE Go, LLC's (SVE) "ex parte application to vacate and declare void the right to attach order [RTAO] entered on 7/8/2024" is granted. However, the RTAO is hereby re-issued effective July 30, 2024.

On Friday, July 5, 2024, this court posted a tentative ruling granting plaintiff's motion for an RTAO. That same day, SVE filed removal papers in federal court. Early on Monday, July 8, unaware of the putative removal, I granted plaintiff's motion for RTAO. On July 23, the federal court remanded the case, finding "the circumstances and timeline of this removal strongly suggest that the removal was frivolous and designed to impede the state court proceedings."

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and attaches a letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare and submit a new right to attach (proposed) order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
